text:
There's a lake in the woods that attracts a lot of wild hares. The locals would feed them carrots, berries—they're big, fluffy things, affectionate creatures. One morning there was an old hunter who wasn't accustomed to these pet hares. He sneaked up one day to fill his bottle.

"Oh dear," the hare thought. "That hunter seems quiet."

Quietly he tiptoed toward the edge of the water. The hunter seemed deep in concentration, sketching something into his book.

Suddenly, the hare saw his shadow creeping across the-water's surface. The old man didn't move.

That's when the hare leaped out, chased by a rabbit who thought it could snatch those drawings. They rolled together across the water, a blur of hooves.

You wouldn't believe it. That little hare taught every forest bird to recognize the old painter.

The painter would put his pencil into the water fountain.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
